Kemal Özkırşehirli

B.Sc. candidate, Computer Science and Engineering, MIT

Physics: Chemical Physics · Mathematics: Mathematical Biology

Minors in Philosophy and Writing · GPA 5.0/5.0

Principal Investigator, Özkırşehirli Group

Research across MIT CSAIL, MIT Chemistry, and MIT Physics

Teaching Fellow, MIT EECS

International student from İstanbul, Türkiye · expected May 2027 · graduating one year early

Cambridge, MA, USA - kemalozk@mit.edu (contact)

Awards:

  • Bronze Medal, 56th International Chemistry Olympiad
  • Bronze Medal, 58th International Mendeleev Chemistry Olympiad
  • Silver Medal, TÜBİTAK National Chemistry Olympiad
  • Columbia Science Research Fellow and Likely Letter recipient
  • World Science Scholar
  • University of Waterloo CHEM13 distinction
  • AIME qualification and AMC Hall of Fame recognition

My work and the Özkırşehirli Group aim to turn difficult scientific systems into computable representations, testable algorithms, and decision-making tools for discovery. (1) We develop molecular-simulation and CADD workflows that combine docking, free-energy methods, electronic-structure calculations, and uncertainty-aware ranking. (2) We build and evaluate protein, antibody, genome, and immune-state models, including generative and diffusion systems, with emphasis on reliability, calibration, and mechanistic validity. (3) We use geometric deep learning, statistical mechanics, and stochastic simulation to recover structure, dynamics, and governing rules from complex biological and physical systems. (4) We integrate these methods into auditable scientific workflows for drug discovery, protein design, target discovery, and biomolecular modeling.

Professional

About me - Professional - Education - Awards - Leadership - Contact - CV

Research - 3D Meshes - Protein Safety - CADD - Chromatin - Target Discovery - Quantum/Stat Mech

AI/ML for Science - Selected Work

Molecular Simulation, CADD, and Chemical Physics

TBXT - Chordoma/TBXT CADD sprint; docking, GNINA CNN rescoring, QSAR, BRICS generation, Boltz-2 co-folding, MMGBSA/FEP scaffolding, paralog selectivity, and public research release

ChemAgent-QSM - LLM-guided quantum/statistical-mechanics pipelines using PySCF; TCFs, local order, relaxation, mobility, optimized geometries, and spectra

Renormalization Group - GNN/PyTorch Kadanoff RG framework for spin systems, fixed points, phase boundaries, and statistical-mechanics simulation [code soon-to-be released]

Kupcinet-Getz - Computational biochemical diffusion at Weizmann; stochastic reaction-diffusion modeling, PINN/neural-ODE solvers, Gillespie SSA, nonlinear oscillations, and trajectory analysis [code soon-to-be released]

Buchwald Lab - Advanced organic synthesis in MIT Chemistry; CuH-catalyst development, asymmetric methylation/alkylation, NMR/HPLC analysis, and substrate-scope expansion

Pedal AI - Python/LangGraph backend and chemistry-agent workflow engineering; stateful tool orchestration, molecular-prediction API integration, retrosynthesis-route UX, and cheminformatics workflow design [code not public]

Sepal AI / Mercor - Chemistry LLM evaluation, rubric design, retrosynthesis/reaction-mechanism task construction, prompt analysis, and failure-mode annotation

DFT → kMC - Multi-scale computational organic chemistry pipeline; transition-state screening, reaction-trajectory analysis, solvent-pathway modeling, and predicted yield optimization [code soon-to-be released]

Coursework and Toolchain

Stack - Python, C/C++, CUDA, HPC, PyTorch, RDKit, OpenMM/OpenFE, GNINA/Vina, Boltz-2, PySCF, LangGraph, FastAPI, LaTeX

Chem/Chemical Physics/Simulation - 8.592[J] Statistical Physics in Biology; 2.094[J] Atomistic Modeling and Simulation of Materials and Structures; 8.05 Quantum Physics II; 5.601 Thermodynamics I; 5.351 Fundamentals of Spectroscopy; 5.43 Advanced Organic Chemistry

CS/AI/Systems - 6.7960 Deep Learning; 18.404[J] Theory of Computation; 6.5240 Sublinear Time Algorithms; 6.4610 Natural Language Processing; 6.3930 AI and Decision Making in Medicine: From Disease to Therapy; 6.1220[J] Advanced Algorithm Design-Analysis; 6.1910 Computer Structures and Architectures; 6.1903 Introduction to Low-level Programming in C and Assembly; 6.100B Introduction to Computational Thinking and Data Science; 18.615 Stochastic Processes; 6.1200[J] Discrete Mathematics; 18.600 Probability Theory and Random Variables; 18.06 Linear Algebra

Humanitas Core - 24.245 Theory of Models; 24.121 Metaphysics; 24.251 Introduction to Philosophy of Language; 17.01[J]/24.04[J] Justice; 24.05 Philosophy of Religion; 21W.747 Rhetoric; 21W.745 Advanced Essay Workshop; 21W.762 Poetry Workshop; 21W.738[J] Memoir

Columbia Coursework - CHEM UN2045 Intensive Organic Chemistry I; COMS W3137 Data Structures and Algorithms; MATH UN1205 Accelerated Multivariable Calculus; PHYS UN1401 Introduction to Mechanics and Thermodynamics; PHYS UN1402 Introduction to Electricity, Magnetism, and Optics; ELEN E1201 Introduction to Electrical Engineering and Lab; ENGL CC1010.2xx University Writing: Readings in Gender and Sexuality; HUMA CC1001–CC1002 Literature Humanities I & II; SCNC CC1000/CC1100 Frontiers of Science

Biomolecular AI, Protein, and Genome Modeling

3D Meshes - Principal-investigator-led methodology-first geometric deep learning collaboration for 3D meshes and point clouds, with a Google-affiliated research lead; appropriate life-science applications include topology of enzymes, antibodies, and other biomolecular complexes [code soon-to-be released]

ChromoGen V2 - Conditional diffusion for small-molecule-perturbed single-cell 3D chromatin ensembles with Plate-C/Easy Dip-C/CHARM-style data [code soon-to-be released]

PRISM - Adversarial evaluation, reliability checks, uncertainty calibration, and failure taxonomies for protein foundation models [code soon-to-be released]

V2M - Autoimmune genetics-to-mechanism target-discovery engine with single-cell foundation models, QTL/colocalization, targetability checks, and therapeutic-control validation [code soon-to-be released]

EVEdesign - 21-person, 18-institution, 8-country protein-design collaboration; uncertainty-aware multi-objective candidate selection and sequence-structure-function objectives [code soon-to-be released]

Antibody RL - Structure-informed cross-attention transformer and PPO pipeline with OAS/SAbDab/IEDB data and AF-Multimer/IGFold-style metrics

Education and Awards

MIT - triple major in CS&E, Chemical Physics, Mathematical Biology; minors in Philosophy and Writing; GPA 5.0/5.0; expected May 2027, on track to graduate a year early

Columbia - Science Research Fellow and Likely Letter recipient; BA study in Chemistry and CS-Mathematics before transferring to MIT

Olympiads - IChO bronze, Mendeleev bronze, TÜBİTAK silver, Istanbul chemistry gold, Lavoisier gold, Waterloo distinctions

AMC/AIME - AIME qualifier and AMC Hall of Fame acknowledgement; 7th place in Türkiye and top 5% AMC 12 score

YC / WSS - YC Startup School 2026 and World Science Scholar recognition

Leadership and Community

MIT BioMakers - dry-lab member in a biomaking community for interdisciplinary projects, workshops, and student-led research infrastructure

Schwarzman UAG - undergraduate advisory group context for student input into computing education and research at MIT

Undergraduate Association - MIT undergraduate student-government and Institute-committee representation context

AI@MIT - AI and machine-learning undergraduate community; protein/antibody discovery collaboration context

Formula SAE - PCB systems, embedded software, sensor integration, and low-voltage microelectronics for electric racing

Math Journal - content editor and referee producing LaTeX reports on undergraduate mathematics theses and papers

Olympiad Captain - mentored 50-member chemistry olympiad team; 35 members placed in top rankings; organized practical training

Volleyball - team captain and primary setter; coordinated training, team culture, and service activities
